title: hibbertia subglabra
intro: Hibbertia subglabra, also known as the yellow buttercup, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to eastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Hibbertia subglabra include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Hibbertia subglabra can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of a salve, infusion, or tincture, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken orally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
